titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 567

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=876 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.600047+00 | translation moa=877 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.610231+00

p.cl: . DD . dei gratia rex scottorum . omnibus per regnum suum in scottia et Lodoneio constitutis scottis et anglis  salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse et dedisse deo
et Sancto Cuthberto et monachis eius in elemosinam has terras in lodoneio . scilicet Goldingeham . Aldecambes . Lummesdene . Ristun . Reinintun . Swinewde
prenegest . Eitun . et aliam Eitun . et Cramesmutham . Lambertun . et aliam Lamberun . Paxtun . Fiswic . et Swintun . et omnes has superscriptas terras ego do et concedo
deo et Sancto predicto et monachis eius cum sacca et socna . et toll et team . et Infangethef . et cum omnibus terris et siluis . et aquis . et fracturis
nauium . et cum omnibus consuetudinibus liberas et quietas . ab omni opere et seruitio . pro salute anime mee . et filii mei Henrici . et pro animabus patris
et matris mee . et fratrum et sororum mearum . Precipio etiam et defendo ne aliquis de hac mea donatione aliquam iniuriam uel molestiam
aut calumpniam monachis sancti cuthberti amodo faciat . quia uolo ut hec mea elemosina libera et quieta ab omni calumpnia imperpetuum
remaneat .
Hec carta firmata est anno ab incarnatione domini . m . c . xxvi . tercio anno regni mei aput pebles .

teste . et concessu Henrici
filii mei . et isti alii sunt inde testes . Iohannes episcopus . Rodbertus de brus . Herbertus cancellarius . ascelinus archidiaconus . Paganus de braiosa .
hugo brito . Berengarius ingania . Gospatricus . vicecomes . aimarus .
David by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all throughout his kingdom settled in Scotia and Lothian, Gael and English, greeting.

Know me to have conceded, and given in alms to God, Saint Cuthbert and his monks, these lands in Lothian, that is to say, Coldingham, Old Cambus, Lumsdaine, Reston, Renton, Swinewood, Prenderguest, Ayton and the other Ayton, and Cramsmoo, Lamberton and the other Lamberton, Paxton, Fishwick, and Swinton. All these lands written above I give and concede to God and to the aforesaid saint and to his monks with sake and soke, toll and team, infangtheif, and with all lands, woods, waters, and shipwrecks, and with all customs, free and quit from every work and service, for the salvation of my own soul and of my son Henry, and for the souls of my father and mother, and my brothers and sisters. I also enjoin and forbid that anyone cause any injustice, trouble, or challenge in respect of this my donation to the monks of Saint Cuthbert henceforward, since I will that this my alms remain free and quit from every claim for ever.
This charter has been made firm in the year 1126 after the Lord's incarnation, in the third year of my reign, at Peebles,

by the witness and consent of Henry my son. And these others are witnesses of it: John the bishop, Robert de Brus, Herbert the chancellor, Ascelin the archdeacon, Payn de Briouze, Hugh Brett, Berengar Engaine, Cospatric the sheriff, Aimer.
